questions.stem: What is the top-level container for all Google Cloud resources in an organization's hierarchy?

options.explanation: Correct. The organization is the root of the resource hierarchy.
Wrong. Folders are below the organization.
Wrong. Billing accounts are separate from the resource hierarchy root.

questions.stem: Which resource groups related Google Cloud services and forms the basic billing/permission boundary?

options.explanation: Correct. Projects are the fundamental organizing and billing unit.
Wrong. Folders group projects, not services directly.
Wrong. A VPC is a networking construct.

questions.stem: Which tool is the command-line interface for Google Cloud?

options.explanation: Correct. gcloud manages Google Cloud resources from the command line.
Wrong. kubectl targets Kubernetes, not all of Google Cloud.
Wrong. Terraform is third-party IaC, not the native CLI.
Wrong. gsutil is specific to Cloud Storage.

questions.stem: Which command-line tool historically manages Cloud Storage buckets and objects?

options.explanation: Correct. gsutil/gcloud storage manage Cloud Storage.
Wrong. bq is the BigQuery CLI.
Wrong. cbt is the Cloud Bigtable CLI.

questions.stem: Which structure lets you group projects for shared policy in large organizations?

options.explanation: Correct. Folders organize projects for inherited policy.
Wrong. Those are identities for workloads.

questions.stem: How is a billing account associated with resources?

options.text: It is linked to one or more projects to pay for their usage
options.explanation: Correct. Projects link to a billing account for charges.

options.text: It is attached directly to each VM
options.explanation: Wrong. Billing is at the project level, not per VM.
Wrong. Billing accounts are separate from the hierarchy root.

options.text: It is stored inside a bucket
options.explanation: Wrong. Billing accounts are not objects in storage.

questions.stem: Which compute option provides configurable virtual machines (IaaS)?

options.explanation: Correct. Compute Engine offers customizable VMs.
Wrong. Functions are serverless event handlers.
Wrong. BigQuery is a data warehouse.

questions.stem: Which service runs containers in a fully managed Kubernetes environment?

options.explanation: Wrong. Functions are serverless functions, not Kubernetes.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is a managed relational database.

questions.stem: Which serverless option runs stateless containers that scale to zero?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Run runs containers serverlessly and scales to zero.
Wrong. Compute Engine VMs do not scale to zero like serverless.

questions.stem: Which storage class is cheapest for data accessed less than once a year?

options.explanation: Correct. Archive is the lowest-cost class for rarely accessed data.
Wrong. Standard is for frequently accessed data.
Wrong. Nearline targets roughly monthly access.
Wrong. Coldline targets roughly quarterly access, pricier than Archive.

questions.stem: Which storage class suits data accessed about once a month?

options.explanation: Correct. Nearline is designed for ~monthly access.
Wrong. Standard is for frequent access.
Wrong. Archive is for yearly-or-less access.

questions.stem: Which managed service provides a relational database (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server)?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud SQL is managed relational database hosting.
Wrong. Firestore is a document NoSQL database.
Wrong. BigQuery is an analytics warehouse.

questions.stem: Which database offers global, horizontally scalable, strongly consistent relational storage?

options.explanation: Correct. Spanner is globally distributed, strongly consistent relational.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is regional and does not scale globally like Spanner.
Wrong. Bigtable is NoSQL wide-column, not relational SQL.

questions.stem: Which command creates a Compute Engine VM instance?

options.explanation: Correct. That command provisions a VM.
Wrong. That deploys to Cloud Run.
Wrong. That makes a storage bucket.

questions.stem: Which feature scales a managed instance group based on load?

options.text: Autoscaling on a managed instance group
options.explanation: Correct. MIG autoscaling adds/removes VMs per metrics.
Wrong. One VM does not autoscale horizontally.
Wrong. Buckets do not run compute.

questions.stem: Which feature deploys identical VMs from a common template?

options.text: A managed instance group with an instance template
options.explanation: Correct. MIGs use templates for identical VMs.
Wrong. Functions are serverless, not VM groups.

questions.stem: Which command deploys a container image to Cloud Run?

options.explanation: Correct. gcloud run deploy ships a service to Cloud Run.
Wrong. That loads data into BigQuery.

questions.stem: Which service deploys infrastructure declaratively as configuration on Google Cloud?

options.explanation: Correct. These provision Google Cloud resources declaratively.
Wrong. Monitoring observes; it does not provision.

questions.stem: Which command sets the active project for gcloud commands?

options.explanation: Correct. That sets the default project in the active config.
Wrong. That switches Kubernetes context, not gcloud project.

questions.stem: Which service collects metrics, dashboards, and uptime checks?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Monitoring provides metrics, dashboards, and alerts.

questions.stem: Which service centralizes log collection and analysis?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Logging ingests and lets you query logs.
Wrong. Metrics are numeric; logs are richer records in Cloud Logging.
Wrong. Storage holds objects, not a log query engine.

questions.stem: Which feature notifies you when a metric breaches a threshold?

options.text: An alerting policy in Cloud Monitoring
options.explanation: Correct. Alerting policies trigger notifications on conditions.

questions.stem: Which tool helps diagnose latency by tracing requests across services?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Trace captures distributed latency traces.
Wrong. That is a managed database.

questions.stem: Which approach gives a quick view of a VM's CPU and disk metrics?

options.text: Cloud Monitoring dashboards/metrics for the instance
options.explanation: Correct. Monitoring surfaces instance performance metrics.

questions.stem: Which system controls who can do what on Google Cloud resources?

options.explanation: Correct. IAM governs permissions via roles and bindings.

questions.stem: Which IAM concept is a collection of permissions you grant to a principal?

options.explanation: Correct. Roles bundle permissions granted to principals.

questions.stem: Which identity is used by applications/VMs rather than humans?

options.explanation: Correct. Service accounts are non-human identities for workloads.
Wrong. User accounts are for humans.

questions.stem: Which practice follows least privilege when assigning IAM roles?

options.text: Grant predefined or custom roles scoped to the needed permissions
options.explanation: Correct. Narrow roles limit access to what is required.

options.text: Grant the Owner role to everyone
options.explanation: Wrong. Owner is overly broad and risky.

options.text: Share one service account key widely

questions.stem: Which mechanism restricts inbound/outbound traffic to VMs in a VPC?

options.explanation: Correct. Firewall rules allow/deny traffic to instances.
Wrong. IAM governs API permissions, not packet filtering.

questions.stem: Which feature manages encryption keys you control for Google Cloud services?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud KMS lets you manage CMEK.

questions.stem: Which networking resource provides a global private network for your resources?

options.explanation: Correct. VPCs provide private, global networking in Google Cloud.

questions.stem: Which load balancer distributes global HTTP(S) traffic across regions?

options.explanation: Correct. It routes HTTP(S) traffic globally to the nearest healthy backend.

options.text: An internal TCP load balancer only
options.explanation: Wrong. Internal LBs are regional and not global HTTP(S).
Wrong. One VM is not a load balancer.
Wrong. DNS resolves names; it is not a load balancer.

questions.stem: Which command lists the VMs in your current project?

options.explanation: Correct. That lists Compute Engine instances.
Wrong. That lists Kubernetes nodes, not GCE VMs directly.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you define a target uptime and measure error budgets?

options.explanation: Correct. SLOs track reliability against targets and error budgets.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you grant temporary access without permanent role bindings via short-lived credentials?

options.explanation: Correct. Impersonation issues short-lived credentials for temporary access.
Wrong. Long-lived keys are a leak risk.
Wrong. That is broad and permanent.

questions.stem: Which command authenticates the gcloud CLI with your account?

options.explanation: Correct. gcloud auth login authenticates a user.
Wrong. That shows config, not authentication.

questions.stem: Which service is a serverless, highly scalable data warehouse for analytics?

options.explanation: Correct. BigQuery is a serverless analytics warehouse.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is OLTP relational, not a warehouse.

questions.stem: Which NoSQL service offers low-latency, high-throughput wide-column storage for large analytical/operational workloads?

options.explanation: Correct. Bigtable is a wide-column NoSQL store for large workloads.
Wrong. BigQuery is an analytics warehouse, not low-latency operational NoSQL.

questions.stem: Which command builds and submits a container image using Cloud Build?

options.explanation: Correct. gcloud builds submit runs a Cloud Build job.
Wrong. That runs a BigQuery query.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you export logs to BigQuery or Cloud Storage for retention/analysis?

options.explanation: Correct. Sinks route logs to destinations like BigQuery or Storage.

questions.stem: Which feature defines guardrails that restrict resource configurations org-wide?

options.explanation: Correct. Org policies set constraints across the hierarchy.
Wrong. Firewall rules filter traffic, not resource config.

questions.stem: Which command creates a new project with a specified ID?

options.explanation: Correct. That creates a new project.
Wrong. That selects an existing project, not creates one.

questions.stem: Which option provides persistent block storage attached to a Compute Engine VM?

options.explanation: Correct. Persistent Disks provide durable block storage for VMs.
Wrong. Buckets are object storage, not block disks.

questions.stem: Which compute discount applies automatically for running a VM a large portion of the month?

options.explanation: Correct. Sustained use discounts apply automatically for long-running VMs.
Wrong. Spot is a separate, interruptible model, not automatic sustained discount.
Wrong. Committed use requires a commitment.
Wrong. Sustained use discounts are automatic.

questions.stem: Which feature reduces VM cost for fault-tolerant, interruptible workloads?

options.explanation: Correct. Spot/preemptible VMs are cheaper but can be reclaimed.
Wrong. Commitments suit steady long-term usage.
Wrong. Sustained discounts apply to steady VMs, not the deepest savings for interruptible jobs.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you visualize and alert on logs by extracting metrics from them?

options.explanation: Correct. Log-based metrics derive numeric metrics from log entries.

questions.stem: Which IAM role type can you tailor with an exact set of permissions?

options.explanation: Correct. Custom roles let you choose specific permissions.
Wrong. Basic roles are broad, not tailored.

questions.stem: Which feature secures secrets like API keys with versioning and access control?

options.explanation: Correct. Secret Manager stores and controls access to secrets.
Wrong. Public storage of secrets is unsafe.

questions.stem: Which interface provides a browser-based shell with the Cloud SDK preinstalled?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud Shell is a browser CLI with tools preinstalled.

questions.stem: Which command creates a Cloud Storage bucket?

options.text: gcloud storage buckets create (or gsutil mb)

options.explanation: Wrong. That makes a BigQuery dataset/table.

questions.stem: Which feature lets policies set at a folder automatically apply to projects within it?

options.text: IAM policy inheritance through the resource hierarchy
options.explanation: Correct. Policies inherit down from organization/folders to projects.
Wrong. Firewall rules filter traffic, not inherit IAM.

questions.stem: Which command shows the currently active gcloud configuration?

options.explanation: Correct. config list shows the active configuration.

questions.stem: Which approach manages multiple gcloud environments (accounts/projects) side by side?

options.explanation: Correct. Multiple configurations let you switch contexts easily.
Wrong. That cannot hold multiple environments cleanly.

questions.stem: Which document-model NoSQL database scales automatically and syncs to clients?

options.explanation: Correct. Firestore is a scalable document NoSQL database.
Wrong. Bigtable is wide-column NoSQL, not document with client sync.

questions.stem: Which service ingests and delivers messages for event-driven, decoupled systems?

options.explanation: Correct. Pub/Sub is a scalable messaging service for decoupling.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is a database.

questions.stem: Which managed service runs Apache Spark and Hadoop jobs?

options.explanation: Correct. Dataproc runs managed Spark/Hadoop clusters.
Wrong. BigQuery is a serverless warehouse, not Spark/Hadoop clusters.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is relational OLTP.

questions.stem: Which service builds serverless data pipelines for stream and batch (Apache Beam)?

options.explanation: Correct. Dataflow runs serverless Beam stream/batch pipelines.
Wrong. Dataproc is cluster-based Spark/Hadoop, not serverless Beam.

questions.stem: Which service provides managed DNS hosting on Google Cloud?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud DNS hosts and serves DNS zones.
Wrong. Cloud SQL is a database.

questions.stem: Which command connects kubectl to a GKE cluster?

options.explanation: Correct. That fetches credentials for kubectl.
Wrong. That makes a BigQuery dataset.

questions.stem: Which command creates a GKE cluster?

options.explanation: Correct. That provisions a GKE cluster.
Wrong. That deploys to Cloud Run.
Wrong. kubectl does not create GKE clusters.

questions.stem: Which GKE mode manages nodes for you so you focus only on workloads?

options.explanation: Correct. Autopilot manages the nodes/infrastructure automatically.
Wrong. Standard requires you to manage node pools.
Wrong. That is not a GKE mode.
Wrong. Cloud Run is a separate serverless product.

questions.stem: Which feature deploys an application across multiple environments using version-controlled config?

options.explanation: Correct. IaC enables repeatable, version-controlled deployments.
Wrong. Manual steps are not repeatable IaC.

questions.stem: Which command copies a local file to a Cloud Storage bucket?

options.explanation: Correct. cp uploads files to a bucket.
Wrong. scp targets VMs, not bucket uploads.

questions.stem: Which feature checks whether a public endpoint is reachable on a schedule?

options.text: An uptime check in Cloud Monitoring
options.explanation: Correct. Uptime checks probe endpoints periodically.

questions.stem: Which feature groups related metrics and charts for at-a-glance health?
Which agent collects system and application metrics/logs from a VM?

options.explanation: Correct. The Ops Agent gathers metrics and logs from VMs.

questions.stem: Which approach helps find which release introduced an error using profiling/diagnostics?

options.explanation: Correct. Profiler and related tools surface performance and error sources.

questions.stem: Which service aggregates and groups application errors automatically?

options.explanation: Correct. Error Reporting groups and surfaces recurring errors.

questions.stem: Which IAM element is the identity you grant a role to (user, group, or service account)?

options.explanation: Correct. Principals are the identities receiving role grants.
Wrong. Permissions are bundled into roles, not the grantee.

questions.stem: Which approach avoids long-lived service account keys for workloads on Google Cloud?

options.text: Attach a service account to the resource (no exported key)
options.explanation: Correct. Attached service accounts avoid exported key files.

options.text: Commit the key to source control
options.explanation: Wrong. Never commit secrets to source control.
Wrong. Public keys are a severe risk.

questions.stem: Which feature controls which external IPs can reach the Google Cloud console/APIs for an org (context-aware)?

options.text: Access Context Manager / VPC Service Controls
options.explanation: Correct. These enforce context-aware access and service perimeters.

questions.stem: Which capability gives a security perimeter around services to mitigate data exfiltration?

options.explanation: Correct. VPC Service Controls create a service perimeter to reduce exfiltration risk.
Wrong. Firewall rules are network filters, not service perimeters.
Wrong. That is narrower than a service perimeter.

questions.stem: Which command links a billing account to a project?

options.explanation: Correct. That associates a project with a billing account.
Wrong. That creates a project, not billing linkage.

questions.stem: Which networking option connects an on-premises network to a VPC over IPsec?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud VPN establishes IPsec tunnels to a VPC.

questions.stem: Which option provides a dedicated, private physical connection to Google Cloud?

options.explanation: Correct. Dedicated Interconnect provides a private physical link.
Wrong. VPN runs over the public internet, not a dedicated circuit.

questions.stem: Which feature lets a VM reach the internet for updates without an external IP?

options.explanation: Correct. Cloud NAT provides outbound internet for private VMs.
Wrong. Firewall rules filter; they do not provide NAT egress.

questions.stem: Which feature allows zero-downtime rollout of a new VM template across a managed instance group?

options.text: A rolling update on the managed instance group
options.explanation: Correct. MIG rolling updates replace instances gradually.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you set a budget and receive alerts as spend approaches it?

options.explanation: Correct. Budgets notify you as costs approach thresholds.

questions.stem: Which approach analyzes detailed billing data with SQL?

options.text: Export billing data to BigQuery and query it
options.explanation: Correct. BigQuery billing export enables SQL cost analysis.

questions.stem: Which IAM feature lets you see who has access to a resource?

options.text: The IAM policy (allow policy) bindings on the resource
options.explanation: Correct. The allow policy lists principals and their roles.

questions.stem: Which tool helps identify overly permissive IAM grants and recommends reductions?

options.explanation: Correct. IAM Recommender suggests least-privilege reductions.

questions.stem: Which command deletes a project (scheduling it for removal)?

options.explanation: Correct. That schedules a project for deletion.
Wrong. That only clears the active project setting.

questions.stem: Which feature reduces cost for a 1- or 3-year predictable Compute Engine commitment?

options.explanation: Correct. Committed use offers discounts for term commitments.
Wrong. Spot is interruptible, unsuitable for steady prod commitments.
Wrong. Sustained discounts apply automatically but are smaller than committed use.

questions.stem: Which storage option suits data accessed roughly once a quarter at low cost?

options.explanation: Correct. Coldline targets ~quarterly access at low cost.
Wrong. Standard is for frequent access.

questions.stem: Which command opens an SSH session to a Compute Engine VM?

options.explanation: Correct. gcloud compute ssh connects to a VM.
Wrong. That deploys to Cloud Run.
Wrong. That runs commands in a pod, not a GCE VM.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you run a startup script when a VM boots?

options.text: A startup script in instance metadata
options.explanation: Correct. Startup scripts run on boot via metadata.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you receive alerts via email, SMS, or webhook?

options.explanation: Correct. Notification channels deliver alerts through various methods.

questions.stem: Which approach helps reduce noisy alerts from transient spikes?

options.text: Tune alert conditions with duration/aggregation windows
options.explanation: Correct. Duration and aggregation reduce false positives.

questions.stem: Which IAM best practice applies to the basic Owner role?

options.text: Avoid broad use; prefer predefined or custom roles
options.explanation: Correct. Owner is very broad; narrower roles follow least privilege.

options.text: Grant Owner to all team members
Use Owner for service accounts by default
options.explanation: Wrong. Workloads should get scoped roles.

options.text: Owner is the most secure choice

questions.stem: Which mechanism lets workloads outside Google Cloud access resources without service account keys?

options.explanation: Correct. It lets external identities access Google Cloud without keys.

options.text: Sharing a service account key file
options.explanation: Wrong. Keys are a leak risk this feature avoids.

questions.stem: Which feature applies labels to resources to organize and break down billing?

options.explanation: Correct. Labels categorize resources, useful for billing breakdowns.

questions.stem: Which service provides an in-memory cache (Redis/Memcached) as a managed offering?

options.explanation: Wrong. Cloud SQL is relational, not an in-memory cache.

questions.stem: Which command shows the gcloud components and lets you update the SDK?

options.explanation: Correct. That updates installed SDK components.

questions.stem: Which feature retains logs longer by routing them to a dedicated log bucket?

options.explanation: Correct. Log buckets let you set retention periods.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you audit who accessed or changed resources?

options.explanation: Correct. Audit Logs record admin and data access activity.

questions.stem: Which service provides a global, autoscaling HTTP backend without managing servers, billing per request?

options.explanation: Correct. These serverless platforms autoscale and bill per use.
Wrong. VMs are always-on and not per-request billed.

questions.stem: Which command grants an IAM role to a principal on a project?

options.explanation: Correct. That binds a role to a member on the project.

questions.stem: Which design improves reliability by spreading a managed instance group across zones?

options.explanation: Correct. Regional MIGs distribute instances across zones for resilience.
Wrong. One zone is a single point of failure.

questions.stem: Which feature lets you require multi-factor verification for Google Cloud sign-in?

options.text: 2-Step Verification / MFA on the Google identity

questions.stem: Which command displays the IAM policy currently set on a project?

options.explanation: Correct. That returns the project's IAM allow policy.
